home.title: Alejandro Ortiz Corro
home.summary: Product engineering for teams that need trust, clarity, and reliable delivery.
home.seoDescription: Alejandro Ortiz Corro designs and builds reliable product systems, public websites, private dashboards, backend workflows, and software architecture for teams that need business-critical software to launch well.

homeContent.headline: Product systems that earn trust and keep moving.
homeContent.deck: I build the layer between a business goal and the software that has to carry it: fast public pages, private operating tools, and backend workflows that stay observable after launch.
homeContent.proofBoardTitle: One public sample, with the private seams kept honest.
homeContent.proofBoardBody: A system view for how AOHYS separates public trust, private work, releases, leads, and observability.
homeContent.selectedOutcomesIntro: A fast pass through public-safe evidence: live work, active builds, confidential enterprise delivery, and what each one says about the way I build.

selectedOutcomes.title: Casa Roca

homeContent.architectureHeading: Quality should be visible before anyone has to ask.
homeContent.architectureBody: The public pages, private dashboard, contact path, analytics, and release flow are separated so search, privacy, delivery, and failures can be reviewed before a client or hiring team has to discover the issue for me.

architectureStages.label: Public surface
architectureStages.text: Astro pages resolve from stable content IDs, localized slugs, canonical URLs, and sitemap rules.

architectureStages.label: Private surface
architectureStages.text: Dashboard routes stay out of public search and will use authenticated workflows instead of public content shortcuts.

architectureStages.label: Release path
architectureStages.text: Feature branches move into develop first; production promotion is a separate review path into main.

homeContent.practiceBody: I do not start by selling a stack. I map the workflow, decide the boundaries, and keep the release path visible enough that quality can be inspected.

practicePoints.label: Clarify the flow
practicePoints.text: Name the business goal, constraints, and operating reality before choosing an implementation shape.

practicePoints.label: Design the operating system
practicePoints.text: Make UI, data, auth, communication, observability, and deployment fit together instead of treating them as disconnected chores.

practicePoints.label: Verify before launch
practicePoints.text: Use tests, browser QA, protected branches, preview checks, and production verification so failures are visible early.

homeContent.contactHeading: Tell me what needs to work better.
homeContent.contactBody: Send the role, project, architecture problem, or product flow. I will reply with a practical next step.

case-studies.title: Selected work
case-studies.summary: A public-safe view of shipped work, active products, and confidential enterprise delivery.
case-studies.seoDescription: Review selected AOHYS work across production websites, active product builds, business systems, and enterprise software delivery.

case-study:casa-roca.title: Casa Roca
case-study:casa-roca.summary: Production proof for a public business site with careful SEO, performance, and deployment discipline.
case-study:casa-roca.seoDescription: Casa Roca is production evidence for public business site delivery, SEO readiness, performance care, and release discipline.

caseStudyContent.overview: Casa Roca is a live hospitality website for a boutique stay in Roca Partida, Veracruz. The public case study focuses on trust, discoverability, performance-minded delivery, and the separation between public marketing pages and private operations.

problem.title: Problem
problem.body: The business needed a public web presence that could communicate place, experience, and credibility without exposing the operational systems behind reservations, media management, and private administration.

businessOutcome.title: Business outcome
businessOutcome.body: A production website gives Casa Roca a public destination for guests, search traffic, brand trust, and direct inquiry paths while keeping the operational back office separate from the public proof surface.

role.title: Role
role.body: Public site delivery, bilingual surface planning, SEO metadata, release verification, performance care, and a clear boundary between public pages and private operational workflows.

constraints.title: Constraints
constraints.body: The case study can show the live public website and architecture decisions, but it must avoid private booking data, admin dashboard details, credentials, internal code, and operational records.

architectureDecisions.title: Architecture decisions
architectureDecisions.body: Keep public pages fast and inspectable, treat bilingual content and SEO metadata as first-class concerns, and separate public evidence from private business workflows so the site can be presented safely.

executionHighlights.title: Execution highlights
executionHighlights.body: The work emphasized clear hospitality positioning, localized public routes, production deployment discipline, content safety, and a public evidence path that can be reviewed without privileged access.

qualitySecurityPerformance.title: Quality, security, and performance
qualitySecurityPerformance.body: The public surface is verified as live production evidence, avoids sensitive operational data, uses explicit metadata, and is framed around fast, stable, search-readable pages.

publicEvidence.label: Production website
publicEvidence.description: Live public Casa Roca website used as production-safe evidence.
publicEvidence.altText: Public-safe evidence for the Casa Roca production website

confidentialityNote.title: Confidentiality note
confidentialityNote.body: Only the public website and public-safe delivery context are shown here. Private client code, booking records, admin workflows, credentials, analytics, and operational data remain private.

case-study:the-barber-central.title: The Barber Central
case-study:the-barber-central.summary: An active booking and operations product shaped around local business workflows.
case-study:the-barber-central.seoDescription: The Barber Central shows active product architecture for booking, operations, payments, and customer communication workflows.

caseStudyContent.overview: The Barber Central is an active product build for service businesses that need booking, operations, customer communication, and payments to work as one system.

problem.title: Problem
problem.body: Local service businesses often run bookings, reminders, staff operations, and payments across disconnected tools, which makes growth harder and creates avoidable manual work.

businessOutcome.title: Business outcome
businessOutcome.body: The product direction centers the daily operating workflow: book clients, manage availability, communicate clearly, and keep the business moving without stitching tools together by hand.

role.title: Role
role.body: Product architecture, workflow modeling, full-stack implementation planning, booking flow decisions, payments readiness, customer communication, and quality gates for an active build.

constraints.title: Constraints
constraints.body: The build is still in development, so public proof must avoid private business data, unfinished dashboard internals, secrets, and customer records while still showing the product judgment.

architectureDecisions.title: Architecture decisions
architectureDecisions.body: Use a modern full-stack app shape with a realtime backend, authenticated dashboard boundaries, graph-backed public narrative, and provider integrations treated as explicit operational seams.

executionHighlights.title: Execution highlights
executionHighlights.body: The work covers booking IA, operations dashboard shape, provider setup, environment discipline, and manual browser QA for flows where auth, origin, and preview behavior matter.

qualitySecurityPerformance.title: Quality, security, and performance
qualitySecurityPerformance.body: The project emphasizes protected environments, explicit verification commands, browser QA, source checks, and careful handling of auth/provider configuration before public launch.

publicEvidence.label: Development preview
publicEvidence.description: Public-safe summary of an active product build; screenshots and preview URLs can be added after final sanitation.
publicEvidence.altText: Public-safe development preview summary for The Barber Central

confidentialityNote.title: Confidentiality note
confidentialityNote.body: Only the public-safe product direction is shown. Private implementation details, secrets, test data, customer records, provider dashboards, and unpublished business information stay private.

case-study:nutri-plan.title: Nutri Plan
case-study:nutri-plan.summary: An active product system for client workflows, plans, and operational clarity.
case-study:nutri-plan.seoDescription: Nutri Plan shows active product work around client workflows, structured plans, operational tools, and delivery quality.

caseStudyContent.overview: Nutri Plan is a private product system for nutrition workflows, dashboards, mobile access, and operational clarity across several surfaces.

problem.title: Problem
problem.body: Nutrition operations need connected client records, plans, dashboards, and mobile experiences, but the domain becomes fragile when workflow state is split across unrelated tools.

businessOutcome.title: Business outcome
businessOutcome.body: The build organizes the product around real operational roles and shared backend state so the system can support client work, admin work, and mobile access from one model.

role.title: Role
role.body: Product workflow architecture, dashboard planning, backend state modeling, mobile surface coordination, quality practice, and release discipline for a private active build.

constraints.title: Constraints
constraints.body: The product is private, so the public case study can discuss architecture and outcomes only at a safe level without exposing patient-like records, business data, private code, or internal workflows.

architectureDecisions.title: Architecture decisions
architectureDecisions.body: Keep the backend as a shared source of truth, separate public landing concerns from private dashboard/mobile workflows, and make environment and QA gates part of release readiness.

executionHighlights.title: Execution highlights
executionHighlights.body: The work spans landing, dashboard, admin dashboard, mobile app, Convex-backed state, route QA, preview verification, and operational parity with the reference system.

qualitySecurityPerformance.title: Quality, security, and performance
qualitySecurityPerformance.body: The project uses functional QA, route sweeps, environment separation, protected release branches, and preview validation when auth, routing, UI, or behavior changes.

publicEvidence.label: Sanitized preview
publicEvidence.description: Public-safe private-build summary; production screenshots should be sanitized before they become visual evidence.
publicEvidence.altText: Public-safe sanitized preview summary for Nutri Plan

confidentialityNote.title: Confidentiality note
confidentialityNote.body: This case does not publish private product code, client records, health-related data, credentials, private dashboard screens, or operational provider details.

case-study:enterprise-systems.title: Enterprise systems
case-study:enterprise-systems.summary: Confidential work framed through outcomes, architecture decisions, escalation ownership, and delivery practice.
case-study:enterprise-systems.seoDescription: Enterprise systems summarizes confidential software work through business outcomes, architecture judgment, escalation ownership, and delivery practice.

caseStudyContent.overview: Enterprise systems work is presented through public-safe outcomes: escalation ownership, payment plans, cashback, customs workflows, backend delivery, and reliability in complex environments.

problem.title: Problem
problem.body: Large systems accumulate operational edge cases, integration pressure, and support escalations where the cost of unclear ownership is high.

businessOutcome.title: Business outcome
businessOutcome.body: The value is reliable delivery under constraints: untangling escalations, shipping backend workflows, and improving operational paths without exposing confidential employer or client material.

role.title: Role
role.body: Backend delivery, escalation leadership, payment-plan and cashback work, customs workflow implementation, cross-team coordination, and production-minded problem solving.

constraints.title: Constraints
constraints.body: Specific code, incidents, customer data, employer systems, and internal metrics are confidential. The case study must stay at the level of patterns, responsibilities, and outcomes.

architectureDecisions.title: Architecture decisions
architectureDecisions.body: Work from the business workflow backward, isolate risky integration points, keep operational contracts explicit, and favor maintainable backend paths over clever one-off fixes.

executionHighlights.title: Execution highlights
executionHighlights.body: Public-safe examples include payment plan workflows, cashback implementation, customs handling, escalation ownership, and backend delivery across complex organizational contexts.

qualitySecurityPerformance.title: Quality, security, and performance
qualitySecurityPerformance.body: The work depends on careful change management, production awareness, data sensitivity, clear handoffs, and testing around workflows where mistakes become operational risk.

publicEvidence.label: Confidential summary
publicEvidence.description: Public-safe enterprise summary without private employer, customer, incident, or source-code details.
publicEvidence.altText: Public-safe confidential summary for enterprise systems work

confidentialityNote.title: Confidentiality note
confidentialityNote.body: No private employer code, customer data, incidents, internal metrics, credentials, or proprietary architecture details are published in this public case study.

case-study:engineering-practice.title: Engineering practice
case-study:engineering-practice.summary: Modern agent-assisted engineering framed through planning, QA, architecture, observability, deploys, and documentation.
case-study:engineering-practice.seoDescription: Engineering Practice explains Alejandro Ortiz Corro's modern agent-assisted software delivery process across planning, QA, architecture, observability, deployment, and documentation.

caseStudyContent.overview: Engineering Practice is the operating system behind the work: structured discovery, TDD, agent-assisted implementation, browser QA, release discipline, observability, and documentation.

problem.title: Problem
problem.body: Modern software work moves quickly, but speed without architecture, tests, review, and production checks produces fragile systems and weak evidence.

businessOutcome.title: Business outcome
businessOutcome.body: A repeatable engineering process lets projects move faster while still producing inspectable decisions, safer releases, better handoffs, and stronger confidence for business stakeholders.

role.title: Role
role.body: Technical planning, architecture review, TDD flow, agent orchestration, browser QA, release train discipline, source-quality checks, and documentation as part of delivery.

constraints.title: Constraints
constraints.body: The process should demonstrate modern AI-assisted work without pretending every private client project is public or reducing delivery quality to tool choice.

architectureDecisions.title: Architecture decisions
architectureDecisions.body: Use clear seams, graph-backed public content, explicit environment contracts, protected branches, focused tests, and manual browser verification for visible behavior.

executionHighlights.title: Execution highlights
executionHighlights.body: This public site itself is the process evidence: PR review, TDD tracers, Impeccable visual checks, GitHub issue flow, release documentation, and browser QA.

qualitySecurityPerformance.title: Quality, security, and performance
qualitySecurityPerformance.body: Quality includes source checks, type checks, behavior tests, build verification, visual QA, no private data in public routes, and deployment paths that can be audited.

publicEvidence.label: Process evidence
publicEvidence.description: Public source, tests, documentation, and PR history show the engineering workflow behind the site.
publicEvidence.altText: Public-safe process evidence for AOHYS engineering practice

confidentialityNote.title: Confidentiality note
confidentialityNote.body: The process is public where this repository is public. Private client code, private product code, credentials, screenshots, and operational data remain outside this sample.

practice.title: Practice
practice.summary: How modern AI-assisted engineering, quality checks, and release discipline shape the work.
practice.seoDescription: AOHYS practice explains modern AI-assisted engineering, test discipline, review loops, quality gates, and release habits.

architecture.title: Architecture
architecture.summary: How this public sample separates search pages, private operations, delivery checks, leads, and observability.
architecture.seoDescription: Architecture notes for the AOHYS public source sample: Astro SEO pages, private dashboard boundaries, Cloudflare deploys, Convex workflows, and PostHog observability.

architectureContent.heading: Public source sample, private client work.
architectureContent.deck: This site is intentionally inspectable: public search pages, bilingual routing, release documentation, dashboard boundaries, and provider seams are visible in the source. Client and product code stays private.

sections.title: Public surface
sections.body: Astro owns the public SEO pages. The route map comes from stable content IDs, localized slugs, canonical metadata, language alternates, sitemap eligibility, and robots behavior instead of scattered page constants.

sections.title: Private boundary
sections.body: The dashboard belongs on the same domain, but it is a different surface: authenticated, omitted from the sitemap, and noindex. The public site can demonstrate the boundary without implying private work is public.

sections.title: Release Train
sections.body: Feature branches target develop first. Production moves through a separate main promotion path with Cloudflare/Wrangler checks, environment validation, and smoke verification before the canonical domain changes.

sections.title: Environment Contract
sections.body: Local, preview, and production settings are treated as an explicit contract. Provider dashboards may help setup, but durable deploy-time values should reconcile back to GitHub Environments.

sections.title: Public Content Graph
sections.body: The graph is the seam between content and routes. It owns locale variants, public paths, SEO metadata, sitemap behavior, safe evidence labels, and future dashboard publishing invariants.

sections.title: Provider responsibilities
sections.body: Cloudflare handles hosting and media delivery, Convex owns application state and dashboard workflows, PostHog owns analytics and errors, and Resend handles lead notifications. Technology appears as evidence, not as the sales pitch.

architectureContent.boundaryNote: This public repository is a working sample of AOHYS engineering standards. It does not make private client code, product code, credentials, screenshots, or operational data public.

summary: Senior Frontend Developer with 9+ years of experience specializing in React, TypeScript, Next.js, product architecture, performance, SEO, and maintainable UI systems.
Proven delivery across enterprise systems, startup products, healthcare workflows, e-commerce platforms, and public business sites, including systems serving 30.5M+ monthly users.
Experienced with Next.js as a backend surface, backend workflow delivery, escalation ownership, payment plans, cashback, customs workflows, and cross-functional product execution.
Modern AI-assisted engineering practitioner using agents, TDD, browser QA, release discipline, observability, and documentation to turn ambiguous goals into reliable software.

privacy.title: Privacy
privacy.summary: How AOHYS treats contact data, analytics, errors, and private project information.
privacy.seoDescription: AOHYS privacy route explaining contact data, analytics, error reporting, private project boundaries, and public source limits.

privacyContent.heading: Privacy
privacyContent.intro: This site is public source as an engineering sample, but private client work and operational data stay private.

sections.title: Contact requests
sections.body: When you send the contact form, AOHYS uses your name, email, optional phone/company, preferred contact path, intent, and message only to respond to the request.

sections.title: Analytics and errors
sections.body: PostHog is used for explicit pageviews, selected CTA/form events, and browser error signals. Autocapture is disabled, and contact message text is not sent to analytics.

sections.title: Private project boundaries
sections.body: This repository is an inspectable source sample. Private client code, credentials, operational records, and dashboard data are not public.

privacyContent.boundaryNote: The private dashboard under /dashboard is authenticated, noindexed, excluded from the sitemap, and separate from public SEO pages.
